     The Numbers Business Report

John Carter (2012)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $73,058,679Details
International Box Office $209,719,421
Worldwide Box Office $282,778,100
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $25,375,063
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $16,726,604
Total $42,101,667
Further financial details...

Synopsis

"John Carter" - is an action-adventure set on the mysterious and exotic planet of Barsoom. "John Carter" is based on a classic novel by Edgar Rice Burroughs, whose highly imaginative adventures served as inspiration for many filmmakers, both past and present. The film tells the story of war-weary, former military captain John Carter, who is inexplicably transported to Mars where he becomes reluctantly embroiled in a conflict of epic proportions amongst the inhabitants of the planet, including Tars Tarkas and the captivating Princess Dejah Thoris. In a world on the brink of collapse, Carter rediscovers his humanity when he realizes that the survival of Barsoom and its people rests in his hands. 

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$30,180,188 (41.3% of total gross)
Legs:2.42 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:25.8% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$263,700,000 (worldwide box office is 1.1 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,749 opening theaters/3,749 max. theaters, 4.3 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $109,771,583

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: March 9th, 2012 (Wide) by Walt Disney, released as John Carter
March 9th, 2012 (IMAX) by Walt Disney
International Releases: March 7th, 2012 (Wide) (Egypt)
March 8th, 2012 (Wide) (Austria)
March 8th, 2012 (Wide) (Peru)
March 8th, 2012 (Wide) (Serbia and Montenegro)
March 8th, 2012 (Wide) (Argentina)
Video Release: June 5th, 2012 by Buena Vista Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for intense sequences of violence and action.
(Rating bulletin 2204, 1/4/2011)
Running Time: 132 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Development Hell, Intertitle, Invented Language, Motion Capture Performance, Prologue, Voiceover/Narration
Film format: 3-D, 3-D - Post-production Conversion, IMAX: DMR
Geographical setting: Set on Mars
Plot point: Arranged Marriage, Culture Clash, Death of a Son or Daughter, Death of a Spouse or Fiancée / Fiancé, Faked Death, Non-Chronological, Rescue, Surprise Twist, War
Social setting: Civil War
Subgenre: Heroic Adventure
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Science Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Walt Disney Pictures, Jim Morris
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English, Apache

Synopsis

"John Carter" - is an action-adventure set on the mysterious and exotic planet of Barsoom. "John Carter" is based on a classic novel by Edgar Rice Burroughs, whose highly imaginative adventures served as inspiration for many filmmakers, both past and present. The film tells the story of war-weary, former military captain John Carter, who is inexplicably transported to Mars where he becomes reluctantly embroiled in a conflict of epic proportions amongst the inhabitants of the planet, including Tars Tarkas and the captivating Princess Dejah Thoris. In a world on the brink of collapse, Carter rediscovers his humanity when he realizes that the survival of Barsoom and its people rests in his hands.
